states.description: Android app drawer showing installed apps
System dialog asking to set app as default SMS handler
visual_cues: radio buttons for SMS app choice
'Set X as your default SMS app?' title

states.description: In-app screen prompting to set as default SMS app
visual_cues: 'To use Messages, make it your default SMS app' text

states.description: Main inbox showing list of SMS conversations
visual_cues: conversation rows with name/number, preview, timestamp

states.description: Conversation list with one or more threads selected via long-press
visual_cues: contextual action bar with '1 / N' counter
send button (paper plane / SMS) on right

states.description: Bottom sheet to add number to contact
View of a saved contact's profile

visual_cues: editable First name, Last name, Company, Phone, Email fields
radio list: System default, Light, Dark, Dark red, White, Black & White, Custom
empty state or list of keywords

states.description: Dialog to enter a blocked keyword

visual_cues: OTHER section (Privacy policy, Third party licences)

transitions.precondition: app launches and is already default SMS
app not yet default SMS handler

transitions.postcondition: filters list or shows 'No items found'

transitions.action: click('Add a blocked keyword') or click('+')

strategies.name: SEND_NEW_SMS
strategies.preconditions: app open at conversation_list, app is default SMS
strategies.success_signal: sent message appears as outgoing bubble with checkmark below
strategies.fallback: if recipient not confirmed, re-tap the checkmark next to the entered number

strategies.name: REPLY_IN_EXISTING_THREAD
strategies.preconditions: conversation visible in list or via search
strategies.success_signal: new outgoing bubble with the typed text shown in thread
strategies.fallback: if type doesn't register, explicitly tap input field then retype

strategies.name: FIND_CONVERSATION_BY_KEYWORD_OR_NUMBER
strategies.fallback: if 'No items found', go back and scroll list manually, or start new conversation by number

strategies.name: DELETE_THREAD
strategies.success_signal: thread no longer appears in list
strategies.fallback: alternative: open thread, click trash icon in top bar, confirm

strategies.name: BLOCK_SENDER
strategies.success_signal: thread removed from inbox; confirmation toast

strategies.name: SAVE_NUMBER_AS_CONTACT
strategies.success_signal: toast 'X saved'; header may show name; verify via new conversation contact list
strategies.fallback: do NOT use the 'person+' icon at top — it opens add-recipient mode, not save contact

strategies.name: EDIT_CONTACT_FIELDS
steps: click(three_dots) -> 'Details' or open contact via details screen
scroll if keyboard hides target field
strategies.success_signal: updated values visible on contact profile and toast confirmation
strategies.fallback: swipe up to reveal hidden fields when keyboard is open

strategies.name: DELETE_CONTACT
strategies.success_signal: contact disappears from details screen; toast 'contact doesn't exist'

strategies.name: CHANGE_THEME
steps: if Custom: tap 'Primary color' and pick color, OK
strategies.success_signal: section headers and accents adopt selected color
strategies.fallback: dismiss 'app icon customization' warning with OK if tapped by mistake

strategies.name: CHANGE_LANGUAGE
steps: click target language under Suggested list
strategies.success_signal: settings labels switch to selected language

strategies.name: TOGGLE_SETTING_CHECKBOX
steps: scroll to relevant section (e.g. OUTGOING MESSAGES, NOTIFICATIONS)
click target row's checkbox or label

strategies.name: OPEN_ABOUT_AND_READ
steps: optionally click subsection (FAQ / Third party licences / Contributors)
strategies.fallback: the three-dots menu does NOT contain About — use the i icon

strategies.name: QUERY_MESSAGE_TIMESTAMP_OR_CONTENT
steps: locate target conversation (scroll or search by number)
read timestamp shown above target message bubble or text inside bubble
strategies.fallback: the preview in the list often already contains text; verify by opening thread

strategies.name: MARK_AS_UNREAD
strategies.success_signal: option toggles to 'Mark as Read' indicating thread is now unread

dead_ends.note: Cancelling causes the app to close and return to app drawer; must accept default-SMS prompt to access app features
That icon opens 'Add recipients' (group chat) mode; use overflow menu -> 'Add number to contact' instead
The three-dot menu only contains 'Show archived conversations'; Settings is the gear icon and About is the i icon

dead_ends.failed_action: search by full phone number with '+' prefix
dead_ends.note: Search may fail to match phone numbers due to formatting; try without '+' or use number-entry via new-conversation flow
App drawer scrolls vertically only; horizontal swipes do nothing

dead_ends.failed_action: tap default Google Messages icon thinking it's Simple SMS Messenger
dead_ends.note: Wrong app — Simple SMS Messenger has an orange chat-bubble icon, often only in app drawer under 'S'

dead_ends.failed_action: long-press elements without app being default SMS
dead_ends.note: Many actions are gated by being default SMS app; complete default-SMS dialog first
Triggers a warning dialog about launcher compatibility; dismiss with OK and tap 'Theme' instead

categories.name: ADD_ENTRY
categories.precondition: User is in a list-style screen of the relevant data domain (e.g., conversations, contacts, keywords) and has permission/default-app prerequisites satisfied
abstract_steps: From the list screen, invoke the primary 'create' affordance (floating action button or '+' icon)
Provide the required identifying input (recipient, name, keyword) via input field
Confirm/commit the input (checkmark or OK)
If a follow-up form appears, fill remaining required fields
Press the explicit Save/confirm control
failure_modes: Tapping a similar icon that opens a different mode (e.g., add-participant vs add-contact)
Typing while the input field is not focused, so text is dropped
Forgetting to press the final Save/checkmark, leaving changes unpersisted
Dismissing required permission/default-handler prompts and being kicked out of the app
verification_checklist: New entry visibly appears in the list or relevant screen
Confirmation toast or visual cue is shown
Returning to the list and re-opening shows the entry persisted

categories.name: DELETE_ENTRY
categories.precondition: Target item exists and is visible (or reachable via search/scroll) in a list or detail view
abstract_steps: Locate the target item (scroll or search)
Enter selection or detail mode (long-press for list items, open detail for single items)
Invoke the delete affordance (trash icon or overflow -> Delete)
Confirm the destructive action in the confirmation dialog
failure_modes: Confirmation dialog dismissed accidentally, no actual deletion
Selecting the wrong item due to similar names/numbers
Confusing delete-thread with delete-message-content
Repeating confirm taps because UI state did not refresh
verification_checklist: Item no longer appears in the list
Empty-state text or updated count is shown
No success toast indicates pending action; re-check to ensure removal

categories.name: EDIT_ENTRY
categories.precondition: User can navigate to detail/edit screen of the target entity
abstract_steps: Open the entity detail view
Enter edit mode via a pencil/edit affordance
Tap each field to focus, then type the new value
Scroll to reveal fields hidden by the keyboard
Tap the Save control
failure_modes: Editing a similarly-named entity by mistake
Keyboard obscuring fields, leading to no input
Leaving without saving
Field unfocused at moment of typing, so input is lost
verification_checklist: Detail view shows updated values
Confirmation toast appears
Re-entering edit mode shows persisted values

categories.name: QUERY_INFO
categories.precondition: Target information exists somewhere in the app's screens
abstract_steps: Navigate to the most likely container screen (list / detail / about / settings)
Use search or scroll to locate the data
Read the value verbatim from the UI
Return the value in the required format
failure_modes: Reading truncated preview text instead of the full content
Assuming a value from the list when the detail differs
Searching with overly strict formatting (e.g., punctuation) and getting no results
Looking in the wrong menu (overflow vs info icon vs gear)
verification_checklist: Value matches the literal text shown on screen
Format matches what was requested (e.g., HH:MM, full string, comma-separated list)
If multiple matches exist, the correct contextual one was chosen

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO
categories.precondition: User knows the destination screen exists within the app
abstract_steps: From the current screen, identify the likely entry point (top-bar icon, menu item, settings row)
Tap it; if the resulting screen is wrong, back out and try the next candidate
Iterate through known entry points (info icon, gear, overflow) systematically
failure_modes: Confusing icons with similar appearance
Getting trapped in modal dialogs (permissions, defaults) that close the app on cancel
Mis-tapping on adjacent UI elements
verification_checklist: Target screen's distinguishing visual cues are present
Back navigation returns to a known prior screen

categories.name: COMPOSE_AND_SEND_MESSAGE
categories.precondition: Messaging surface available and recipient identifiable
abstract_steps: Open or create the target conversation by recipient identifier
Focus the message input field
Enter the message text
Trigger the send control
Verify the sent indicator (bubble + delivery mark) appears
failure_modes: Sending in the wrong thread because of a similar number
Hitting send before text is committed
Permissions/default-handler prompt blocking the send
Typing into a different focused field (e.g., recipient instead of body)
verification_checklist: Outgoing bubble with exact text appears in the thread
Delivery/sent indicator shown
Recipient header matches target

categories.name: CONFIGURE_PREFERENCE
categories.precondition: User can reach the settings/preferences area of the app
abstract_steps: Open the settings screen
Locate the section containing the preference
Open the picker or toggle for the preference
Select or input the desired value
Confirm/save if required by the screen
failure_modes: Mistaking an adjacent option (e.g., icon color vs theme)
Forgetting to press the final save control
Triggering a warning dialog and not understanding it
verification_checklist: Preference row reflects the new value
Visual effect (color, language) is applied across the UI
Re-entering settings confirms persistence

categories.name: HANDLE_PERMISSION_OR_DEFAULT_HANDLER_PROMPT
categories.precondition: App requires a system role or permission that has not yet been granted
abstract_steps: Read the dialog and identify the required role/permission
Select the correct option (often the target app's own entry)
Press the affirmative confirm button
Wait for app's main UI to load
failure_modes: Pressing cancel/back and getting bounced out of the app
Selecting the wrong app in a chooser
Repeatedly toggling the role and creating loops
verification_checklist: Dialog dismisses and app's main UI is visible
Subsequent actions that depend on the role succeed
